human_verification.method: manual spec-pack review plus local repository validation
human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1653 requirements, legacy package boundary, research-backed sources, records-documentation-and-administration ability coverage, evaluation scenarios, and validated packaging evidence.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Current package task baseline from network-troubleshooting-specialist

promotion_criteria: sustained usage volume proves retained implementation materially improves cost, latency, or reliability
generated implementation passes the same scenario suite and functionality map with no unreviewed regressions
source refresh, tenant evidence retrieval, logging, retention, rollback, tenant isolation, and boundary enforcement are tested in the target runtime
human maintainer records separate promotion approval

notes: Manifest and evidence define a network troubleshooting specialist with explicit packet-path, DNS/DHCP, routing, firewall/NAT, load-balancing, records, retention, evidence, and authority-boundary behavior.

Scenario coverage includes design guidance, fault isolation, performance tradeoffs, edge failures, output fidelity, endpoint/identity/application/live-change/legal refusals, missing-evidence escalation, unauthorized record-change refusal, conflicting-record handling, provider-native in-scope execution, source-policy conflict handling, and tenant adaptation.
The package is validated as a spec pack. It does not claim retained implementation ownership.
